# synology-apm m365 — Microsoft 365 Workload Operations

M365 workloads come in six types: Exchange mailboxes, OneDrive accounts, Teams chats
(`chat`), SharePoint sites, Teams, and M365 Groups. The commands below are shown for
`exchange`; the same commands work for every type — see "Other M365 Workload Types"
at the end of this skill.

Most commands support two modes:

- **Search mode**: pass a `NAME` (mailbox UPN, site name, etc.); the CLI looks it up
  among protected workloads of that type for the resolved tenant (or retired
  workloads, with `--retired`).
- **Direct mode**: pass `--id <uid> --namespace <ns>` to skip the lookup
  (`--tenant-id` is not required in direct mode).

See [apm-shared](../apm-shared/SKILL.md) for global options, output formats, and
confirmation rules.

## Tenant Resolution

Most M365 workload commands accept `--tenant-id`/`-t`. In search mode, omitting it
auto-uses the first connected M365 tenant — fine with a single tenant, ambiguous with
multiple. Use `synology-apm saas list` to find tenant IDs:

### `synology-apm saas list`

List all connected SaaS tenants (M365 + GWS).

```
Usage: synology-apm saas list [OPTIONS]
```

## Commands

### `synology-apm m365 exchange list`

List M365 Workloads of this type.

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange list [OPTIONS]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |
| `--retired` | Show only retired workloads (default: show protected only) |
| `--search <SEARCH>` | Keyword search |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Show only workloads on the specified backup server (get namespace from synology-apm infra server list --verbose) |
| `--plan <PLAN, ...>` | Plan name or ID (repeatable). Resolved against Protection Plans, or Retirement Plans if --retired is set. |

### `synology-apm m365 exchange get`

Show details for an M365 Workload.

**Search mode (searches protected workloads by default):**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange get "alice@contoso.com"
synology-apm m365 exchange get "alice@contoso.com" --retired
```

**Direct mode (--tenant-id not required):**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange get --id <uid> --namespace <ns>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange get [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | UPN (search mode, e.g. alice@contoso.com) |
| `--id <WORKLOAD_ID>` | Workload ID (direct mode; requires --namespace) |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Backup server namespace (direct mode; requires --id) |
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |
| `--retired` | Search in retired workloads (search mode) |

### `synology-apm m365 exchange backup`

Trigger an on-demand backup for an M365 Workload.

**Search mode:**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange backup "alice@contoso.com"
```

**Direct mode (--tenant-id not required):**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange backup --id <uid> --namespace <ns>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange backup [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | UPN (search mode, e.g. alice@contoso.com) |
| `--id <WORKLOAD_ID>` | Workload ID (direct mode) |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Backup server namespace (direct mode) |
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |

### `synology-apm m365 exchange cancel`

Cancel the running backup for an M365 Workload.

**Search mode:**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange cancel "alice@contoso.com"
```

**Direct mode (--tenant-id not required):**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange cancel --id <uid> --namespace <ns>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange cancel [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | UPN (search mode, e.g. alice@contoso.com) |
| `--id <WORKLOAD_ID>` | Workload ID (direct mode) |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Backup server namespace (direct mode) |
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |
| `--yes, -y` | Skip confirmation prompt |

> This action prompts for confirmation. Confirm with the user before running it, and pass `--yes` only once they have explicitly agreed.

### `synology-apm m365 exchange retire`

Retire an M365 Workload (irreversible; requires confirmation).

**Search mode:**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange retire "alice@contoso.com" --plan "Compliance Retention"
```

**Direct mode (--tenant-id not required):**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange retire --id <uid> --namespace <ns> --plan <plan-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange retire [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | UPN (search mode, e.g. alice@contoso.com) |
| `--id <WORKLOAD_ID>` | Workload ID (direct mode) |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Backup server namespace (direct mode) |
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |
| `--plan <PLAN>` | Retirement Plan name or ID (required). Resolved against Retirement Plans. |
| `--yes, -y` | Skip confirmation prompt (destructive, irreversible) |

> **CAUTION:** This action is **irreversible** and normally prompts for confirmation. Confirm with the user before running it, and pass `--yes` only once they have explicitly agreed.

### `synology-apm m365 exchange change-plan`

Change the Protection Plan or Retirement Plan assigned to an M365 Workload.

The plan type --plan is resolved against is auto-detected from the workload's current state: a Protection Plan for an active workload, a Retirement Plan for an already-retired one.

**Search mode:**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange change-plan "alice@contoso.com" --plan "Daily Backup"
synology-apm m365 exchange change-plan "alice@contoso.com" --retired --plan "Compliance Retention"
```

**Direct mode (--tenant-id not required):**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange change-plan --id <uid> --namespace <ns> --plan <plan-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange change-plan [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | UPN (search mode, e.g. alice@contoso.com) |
| `--id <WORKLOAD_ID>` | Workload ID (direct mode) |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Backup server namespace (direct mode) |
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |
| `--retired` | Search in retired workloads (search mode) |
| `--plan <PLAN>` | Plan name or ID (required). Resolved against Protection Plans if the workload is active, or Retirement Plans if it is already retired. |
| `--yes, -y` | Skip confirmation prompt |

> This action prompts for confirmation. Confirm with the user before running it, and pass `--yes` only once they have explicitly agreed.

### `synology-apm m365 exchange version list`

List backup version history for an M365 Workload.

**Search mode:**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange version list "alice@contoso.com"
synology-apm m365 exchange version list "alice@contoso.com" --retired
```

**Direct mode (--tenant-id not required):**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange version list --id <workload-id> --namespace <ns>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange version list [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | UPN (search mode, e.g. alice@contoso.com) |
| `--id <WORKLOAD_ID>` | Workload ID (direct mode) |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Backup server namespace (direct mode) |
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |
| `--retired` | Search in retired workloads (search mode) |
| `--since <SINCE>` | Start time: 30m / 1h / 24h / 7d or ISO 8601 (e.g. 2026-04-01T00:00:00) |
| `--until <UNTIL>` | End time: 30m / 1h / 24h / 7d or ISO 8601 (e.g. 2026-04-20T23:59:59) |

### `synology-apm m365 exchange version get`

Show activity details and logs for a backup version (omit --id to get the latest).

**Search mode (by workload name; omit --id for latest):**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange version get "alice@contoso.com"
synology-apm m365 exchange version get "alice@contoso.com" --id <version-id>
synology-apm m365 exchange version get "alice@contoso.com" --id <version-id> --retired
```

**Direct mode (--tenant-id not required; omit --id for latest):**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange version get --workload-id <wl-id> --namespace <ns>
synology-apm m365 exchange version get --workload-id <wl-id> --namespace <ns> --id <version-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange version get [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | UPN (search mode, e.g. alice@contoso.com) |
| `--id <VERSION_ID>` | Version ID (from version list; omit to get the latest) |
| `--workload-id <WORKLOAD_ID>` | Workload ID (direct mode) |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Backup server namespace (direct mode) |
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |
| `--retired` | Search in retired workloads (search mode) |

### `synology-apm m365 exchange version lock`

Lock a backup version to prevent deletion by retention rules.

**Search mode:**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange version lock "alice@contoso.com" --id <ver-id>
```

**Direct mode (--tenant-id not required):**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange version lock --workload-id <wl-id> --namespace <ns> --id <ver-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange version lock [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | UPN (search mode, e.g. alice@contoso.com) |
| `--id <VERSION_ID>` | Version ID (from version list) |
| `--workload-id <WORKLOAD_ID>` | Workload ID (direct mode) |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Backup server namespace (direct mode) |
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |
| `--retired` | Search in retired workloads (search mode) |

### `synology-apm m365 exchange version unlock`

Unlock a backup version, allowing retention rules to delete it.

**Search mode:**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange version unlock "alice@contoso.com" --id <ver-id>
```

**Direct mode (--tenant-id not required):**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange version unlock --workload-id <wl-id> --namespace <ns> --id <ver-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange version unlock [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | UPN (search mode, e.g. alice@contoso.com) |
| `--id <VERSION_ID>` | Version ID (from version list) |
| `--workload-id <WORKLOAD_ID>` | Workload ID (direct mode) |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Backup server namespace (direct mode) |
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |
| `--retired` | Search in retired workloads (search mode) |

### `synology-apm m365 exchange export cancel`

Cancel an in-progress export task.

**Search mode:**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange export cancel "alice@contoso.com" --id <activity-id>
```

**Direct mode:**

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange export cancel --workload-id <wl-id> --namespace <ns> --id <activity-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange export cancel [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | UPN (search mode, e.g. alice@contoso.com) |
| `--id <ACTIVITY_ID>` | Activity ID (from export list) |
| `--workload-id <WORKLOAD_ID>` | Workload ID (direct mode) |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Backup server namespace (direct mode) |
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |
| `--retired` | Search in retired workloads (search mode) |

### `synology-apm m365 exchange export download`

Start an export and download the PST file, or download an existing export.

Auto-start mode (no --id): starts a new export then downloads when ready.

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange export download "alice@contoso.com"
synology-apm m365 exchange export download "alice@contoso.com" --version-id <vid>
synology-apm m365 exchange export download "alice@contoso.com" --no-wait
```

Direct download mode (--id): downloads a previously started export.

```sh
synology-apm m365 exchange export download "alice@contoso.com" --id <activity-id>
synology-apm m365 exchange export download --workload-id <wl-id> --namespace <ns> --id <activity-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm m365 exchange export download [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | UPN (search mode, e.g. alice@contoso.com) |
| `--id <ACTIVITY_ID>` | Activity ID (from export list) |
| `--filename, -f <FILENAME>` | Output file path (e.g. mailbox.pst); auto-generated from the workload name if omitted |
| `--version-id <VERSION_ID>` | Version ID for auto-start (omit for latest version) |
| `--archive-mailbox` | Export archive mailbox instead of primary (auto-start only; Exchange only) |
| `--export-name <EXPORT_NAME>` | PST filename (auto-generated if omitted; auto-start only) |
| `--no-wait` | Auto-start only: exit immediately if export is not ready; use --id <activity-id> to download later |
| `--workload-id <WORKLOAD_ID>` | Workload ID (direct mode) |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE>` | Backup server namespace (direct mode) |
| `--tenant-id, -t <TENANT_ID>` | Tenant ID (from synology-apm saas list; omit to auto-use the first M365 tenant) |
| `--retired` | Search in retired workloads (search mode) |
| `--yes, -y` | Skip overwrite confirmation prompt |

> This action prompts for confirmation. Confirm with the user before running it, and pass `--yes` only once they have explicitly agreed.

## Other M365 Workload Types

The commands above use `exchange` as the workload type. The same `list` / `get` / `backup` / `cancel` / `retire` / `version ...` commands work for every M365 workload type below — replace `exchange` with the desired type and adjust the NAME argument:

| Workload type | NAME identifier | Export support |
|---|---|---|
| `exchange` (canonical, shown above) | `alice@contoso.com` (mailbox UPN) | Yes (`export list` / `export cancel` / `export download`) |
| `onedrive` | `alice@contoso.com` (user UPN) | No |
| `chat` | `alice@contoso.com` (user UPN) | No |
| `sharepoint` | `HR Site` (site name) | No |
| `teams` | `Engineering` (team name) | No |
| `group` | `marketing@contoso.com` (group email) | Yes (`export list` / `export cancel` / `export download`) |

## Tips

- `synology-apm m365 exchange retire` requires `--plan <archive-plan-id>` — find IDs with
  `synology-apm plan retirement list --verbose`.
- `export download` without `--id` starts a new export and waits for it to finish
  before downloading; pass `--no-wait` to start it and return immediately, then
  download later with `--id <activity-id>` from `export list`.
- `--archive-mailbox` on `export download` only applies to Exchange mailboxes.
